action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 completed training (~458s) with a CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone + same replay recipe. No eval has been submitted for this checkpoint yet, and the IN21k checkpoint from earlier iterations has been overwritten. The final training val_acc (~79.8%) appears slightly lower than the IN21k run (~83%), but test-time accuracy may differ. Analysis: Before deciding whether to revert to IN21k or iterate further, I must verify that the checkpoint contains the correct 'backbone' metadata (so predict.py loads CLIP preprocessing, not IN21k defaults), run a local predict.py sanity check, and submit for evaluation. If CLIP underperforms, I can retrain IN21k quickly (~7.6 min). Decision: Write a quick verification script to inspect/patch the checkpoint if needed, run predict.py on a dummy test subset, then submit the checkpoint for evaluation.
